studyName: DCCT-EDIC Clinical Trial and Follow-up of Persons with Type 1 Diabetes

tables.description: The dataset represents variables that provide information about blood glucose profiles collected at baseline, quarterly visits, and close-out.  Measurements include C-peptide,  creatinine, glucose, and cholesterol (total, HDL, LDL) levels.

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used for a comparison of neuropsychological functions of DCCT volunteers who received standard diabetes treatment to those who received experimental diabetes treatment, at baseline and years 2, 5, 7 and close-out.  Neuropsychological testing focused on cognitive functions (e.g. general intelligence, problem-solving, calculation skills, learning and memory ability), attention and manual dexterity.  Adults were tested with the Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scale and children with the Wechsler Intelligence Scale for children (see also pht000270, ms7exgee; for published results see: <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8554246?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 8554246</a>).

variables.name: CHANGEX2
variables.description: Change: mean of baseline score changes based on clinician's rating -  year 2 (1=significantly improved;2=slightly improved;3=no change;4=slightly worse;5=significantly worse; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the number above [+] or below

variables.name: CHANGEX5
variables.description: Change: mean of baseline score changes based on clinician's rating -  year 5  (1=significantly improved;2=slightly improved;3=no change;4=slightly worse;5=significantly worse; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the number above [+] or below

variables.name: CHNGMAX2
variables.description: Change: highest change from baseline score based on clinician's rating - year 2 (1=significantly improved;2=slightly improved;3=no change;4=slightly worse;5=significantly worse; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the number above [+] or below

variables.name: CHNGMAX5
variables.description: Change: highest change from baseline score based on clinician's rating - year 5  (1=significantly improved;2=slightly improved;3=no change;4=slightly worse;5=significantly worse; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the number above [+] or below

variables.name: GLOBALX2
variables.description: Global: mean of global judgment scores based on clinician's rating - year 2 (1=above average functioning; 2=average functioning; 3=borderline - not definitely pathological; 4=definite mild impairment; 5=definite moderate impairment; 6=definite severe impairment; 7=not ratable; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the above [+] or below

variables.name: GLOBALX5
variables.description: Global: mean of global judgment scores based on clinician's rating - year 5 (1=above average functioning; 2=average functioning; 3=borderline - not definitely pathological; 4=definite mild impairment; 5=definite moderate impairment; 6=definite severe impairment; 7=not ratable; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the above [+] or below

variables.name: GLOBMAX2
variables.description: Global: highest global judgment score received from clinician - year 2 (1=above average functioning; 2=average functioning; 3=borderline - not definitely pathological; 4=definite mild impairment; 5=definite moderate impairment; 6=definite severe impairment; 7=not ratable; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the above [+] or below

variables.name: GLOBMAX5
variables.description: Global: highest global judgment score received from clinician - year 5 (1=above average functioning; 2=average functioning; 3=borderline - not definitely pathological; 4=definite mild impairment; 5=definite moderate impairment; 6=definite severe impairment; 7=not ratable; +/- indicates that the score is thought to be closer to the above [+] or below

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used for a comparison of the occurrence of macrovascular disease and risk factors (e.g. cerebro-vascular disease, coronary arterial disease, peripheral vascular disease, death) in DCCT volunteers randomized to the standard versus those randomized to the experimental group (for published results see <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7732997?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 7732997</a>).  Average follow-up time was 6.5 years.  (1)  Detailed Record of macrovascular events (see also: (2)  Repeated measures of putative risk factors, pht000257, ms5event)

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information at close-out about e.g. diabetes management, adherence to treatment plan, diabetes-related complications, usage of (non-diabetes) medications, general physical and lifestyle measurements (e.g. height, weight, smoking, exercise), medical history of volunteer and family, and socio-economic information (used December '92 to April '93).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect annual information about e.g. diabetes management, adherence to treatment plan, diabetes-related complications, usage of (non-diabetes) medications, general physical and lifestyle measurements (e.g. height, weight, smoking, exercise), medical history, and socio-economic information (used through September '90).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables that provide further information of volunteers deemed eligible following the initial clinic visit (see also pht000249, form 1) about e.g. their diabetes history and management, diabetes-related complications, usage of (non-diabetes) medications, general medical history (including lifestyle measurements such as weight, smoking, exercise), family medical history, and socio-economic information.  Data were collected at eligibility screening.

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information at randomization and at quarterly follow-up visits.  The variables represent information about diabetes management, adherence to treatment plan, diabetes-related complications, usage of (non-diabetes) medications, general physical and lifestyle measurements (e.g. height, weight, smoking, exercise).  (Used through September '90)

tables.description: The dataset represent variables used to compare the effects of standard versus experimental DCCT treatment on the development and/or progression of diabetic neuropathy.  Peripheral nerve measurements (motor and sensory functions, including response amplitude, conduction velocity, F-wave latency, O'Brien score) were taken from median, peroneal and sural nerves, and also included measurements of  autonomic nervous system functions (e.g. R-R variation, orthostatic hypotension, valsalva ratio; for published results see <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7887548?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 7887548</a>).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect annual information about e.g. diabetes management, adherence to treatment plan, diabetes-related complications, usage of (non-diabetes) medications, general physical and lifestyle measurements (e.g. height, weight, smoking, exercise), medical history of volunteers and family, and socio-economic information (used September '90 to November '92).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information at randomization and at quarterly follow-up visits.  The variables represent information about diabetes management, adherence to treatment plan, diabetes-related complications, usage of (non-diabetes) medications, general physical and lifestyle measurements (e.g. height, weight, smoking, exercise).  (Used September '90 to April '93)

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to compare the effects of standard versus experimental treatment on the development and/or progression of diabetic retinopathy.  The retinal health status (e.g. presence/absence of macular edema, microaneurysms, neovascularization, hemorrhages) was monitored through seven-field stereoscopic fundus photographs which were taken bi-annually and graded using the ETDRS scale (for published results see: <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7826293?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 7826293</a>).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables describing HbA1c values, which were used as the main indicator of glycemic control; data were collected at baseline, quarterly, and close-out from participants of the standard group, and at baseline, monthly, and close-out from participants of the experimental group.  Participants who  were attempting to, or had succeeded in becoming pregnant, and those who showed unsafe Hb1Ac levels (i.e. >13.11%), were moved into the experimental group.

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to compare the effects of glycemic exposure observed in DCCT volunteers of the standard versus experimental group on the development and/or progression of diabetic retinopathy.  The retinal health status (e.g. presence/absence of macular edema, microaneurysms, neovascularization) was monitored through seven-field stereoscopic fundus photographs which were taken bi-annually and graded using the ETDRS scale (for published results see: <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7622004?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 7622004</a>).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information about CNS functions, e.g. cognitive and motor functions, including memory, attention, arithmetic, verbal abilities, tactile abilities, and motor coordination.  Data were collected at baseline, years 2, 5, 7 and close-out (see also pht000265, form 13).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to compare the effects of standard versus experimental DCCT treatment on the development and/or progression of diabetic nephropathy (functional status of kidneys determined by, e.g. AER, GFR, creatinine clearance).   Measurements were taken at baseline, quarterly visits, and close-out.  (For published results see <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7643540?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 7643540</a>).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information about volunteers' diets in order to determine if dietary factors might represent confounding effects in the analysis of treatment group assignment and the occurrence of diabetic complications.  The data were collected at baseline, 5-year visit, and close-out; during phase II data were collected on the first annual visit.

variables.name: ALCOHOL
variables.description: Alcohol (gm) - Note: In a number of instances fairly large values were recorded.  Data users should be aware that these values are likely to  represent measurement or data recoding errors.

variables.name: ALCOHOL2
variables.description: Alcohol (xs, i.e. excluding dietary supplements) (gm) - Note: In a number of instances fairly large values were recorded.  Data users should be aware that these values are likely to  represent measurement or data recoding errors.

variables.name: BETACAR2
variables.description: Beta-carotene (xs, i.e. excluding dietary supplements) (mcg) - Note: In a number of instances fairly large values were recorded.  Data users should be aware that these values are likely to  represent measurement or data recoding errors.

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information about pregnancies that occurred during DCCT, their outcome, describing the health of the mother (e.g. presence of infections, preeclampsia, eclampsia), delivery conditions, and health of the newborn (e.g. weight, size, glucose levels).  The data were collected whenever a pregnancy occurred during DCCT (start of collection Jan '91).

tables.description: This dataset represents variables of the SCL-90R questionnaire that are used for an assessment of the emotional status of volunteers (e.g. presence/absence of feelings of anxiety, depression, fear, guilt, heart pounding, headache); information was collected at baseline, annually and close-out.

variables.description: Subject's age

variables.name: AGEGRP
variables.description: Patient's age group (1=adult, 2=adolescent)

tables.description: The information published as 'Adverse Events and their Association with Treatment Regimens in the DCCT' (for published results see: <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8722064?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 8722064</a>) is represented in nine datasets (prefixed with: m9-).  'Adverse events' refers to major accidents, death, hypertension, ketoacidosis, coma, etc., as they occurred in subjects of the standard versus experimental (pump or MDI) treatment group.  M9exall represents data of these adverse events throughout participation in DCCT.

tables.description: The dataset represents data collected for the ms7exf10 dataset (comparison of neuropsychological functions of DCCT patients, comparing those receiving standard to those receiving experimental diabetes treatment; with or with minimal - baseline retinopathy or nephropathy, collected at baseline to that at years 2, 5, 7 and close-out); in ms7exgee the  data were analyzed with a screening algorithm that allows determination of performance changes in comparison to baseline assessment, and identification of volunteers who might be at risk of losing cognitive function (for published results see <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8554246?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 8554246</a>).

tables.description: This dataset represents variables used to assess quality of life (e.g. sleep, diet, time required for treatment regimen, school/work performance) experienced by the volunteer during DCCT; values were assessed at baseline, the 1st and 2nd quarterly endpoint visits, and every six months thereafter.

variables.name: CFAA1
variables.description: How satisfied are you with the amount of time required to manage diabetes (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.name: CFAA2
variables.description: How satisfied are you with the amount of time spent getting check-ups (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.name: CFAA3
variables.description: How satisfied are you with the amount of time it takes to determine sugar levels (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.name: CFAA4
variables.description: How satisfied are you with the current treatment (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.name: CFAA5
variables.description: How satisfied are you with the flexibility of the current diet (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.name: CFAA6
variables.description: How satisfied are you with the burden the current treatment places on family (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.name: CFAA7
variables.description: How satisfied are you with your knowledge about your diabetes (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.name: CFAB1
variables.description: How satisfied are you with your sleep (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.description: How satisfied are you with the appearance of your body (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.description: How satisfied are you with the time spent exercising (see also PMID: 3066604)

variables.description: How satisfied are you with your leisure time (see also PMID: 3066604)

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to obtain information about intercurrent events  (category 1, 2 or 3) whenever they occurred  during the DCCT.  They include description of time, location, type (e.g. ocular, cardiovascular, accidents) and treatment (location, general type, duration) of intercurrent event (see chapter 10 of MOOP).

tables.description: The dataset represents a selection of DCCT-relevant data collected at baseline and includes variables describing e.g. randomization assignment, retinopathy stratum, visual acuity, GFR values, Hb1Ac values, hypoglycemic history, ideal body weight measurements, and quality of life score.

tables.description: The dataset represents variables that provide additional information about the occurrence of hypoglycemic events, e.g. time, location, severity, and queries volunteers about preceding events as potential contributing factors (dietary changes, exercise, menses etc.).  The data were collected whenever severe hypoglycemic events occurred.

variables.name: DUPOK
variables.description: DCCT: NOTE: DCC reports that this variable was used for internal data management and should be ignored by analysts.

variables.name: FORM
variables.description: DCCT: DCCT form number

variables.name: FSASDATE
variables.description: DCCT: Form date as SAS date value

variables.description: DCCT: Patient was alone at onset of symptoms

variables.description: DCCT: Unknown who was with patient at time of symptoms' onset: patient cannot recall

variables.description: DCCT: Person w/patient recognized symptoms of hypoglycemia

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information about volunteers' neurological history and exams (including family history of neuromuscular disorders), in order to determine presence/absence of diabetic neuropathy or similar afflictions which might interfere with participation in the DCCT.  The examination included evaluation of sensory systems, reflex testing, autonomic functions (e.g. postural hypotension, gastroparesis), but also queried for exposure to neurotoxins; information was collected at baseline, year 5 and close-out.

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to obtain information about the occurrence of a hypoglycemic intercurrent event, e.g. time of event, symptoms recognized - by whom, where and how symptoms were treated, and to which treatment group the volunteer belonged.  The data were collected whenever an intercurrent event occurred (starting May ' 85; form 92 was used for additional information starting April ' 86).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to assess the ability of volunteers to perform the tasks required of them as DCCT participants (e.g. testing urine and blood for glucose content several times/24 hours, administering insulin [via injection, pump etc.], following a prescribed diet).  The information was obtained at eligibility screening.

variables.name: DI1ST2ND
variables.description: Administration sequence

variables.name: DIB1
variables.description: How certain (0=Very uncertain; 5=moderately certain; 10=very certain) are you that you will be able to carry out  the following: test urine 3-4 times/day

variables.name: DIB10
variables.description: How certain (0=Very uncertain; 5=moderately certain; 10=very certain) are you that you will be able to carry out  the following: do 3a.m. blood  test once every 3 months

variables.description: How certain (0=Very uncertain; 5=moderately certain; 10=very certain) are you that you will be able to carry out  the following: administer insulin 3-4 times/day

variables.description: How certain (0=Very uncertain; 5=moderately certain; 10=very certain) are you that you will be able to carry out  the following: administer insulin 4 times/day

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to collect information about the health status of median, sural (motor and sensory components) and peroneal (motor component) nerves, e.g. conduction velocity, amplitude, F-wave latency.  The data were collected at baseline, year 5 and close-out.  Volunteers of cohort phase II were also queried at first and second visits.

variables.description: Wrist - abductor pollicis brevis muscle: amplitude (mv)

variables.description: F-wave - wrist: latency (msec)

variables.name: CGB1E
variables.description: Elbow - wrist: temperature before nerve conduction measurement  - Note: There are several instances of obvious errors in recording some body temperatures, e.g., 2 (variable=cgb3d) and 10.1 (variable=cjb1j) degrees centigrade.  Body temperature values outside reasonable ranges (e.g., 30-40 degrees celsius) might be treated as recording or measurement errors.

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to report ratings of neurobehavioral test results (e.g. general intelligence, verbal fluency, learning, attention, abstraction, long-term memory) at baseline, years 2, 5, 7 and close-out (see also <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8021316?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 8021316</a>; <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8554246?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 8554246</a>; pht000270, ms7exgee).  Specifically, results of patients whose test scores appeared to indicate declining cognitive functions were selected to complete this questionnaire (to assure further testing).

tables.description: The information published as 'Adverse Events and their Association with Treatment Regimens in the DCCT' (for published results see: <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8722064?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 8722064</a>) is represented in nine datasets (prefixed with: m9-).  M9exdka is one of four datasets representing variables used to analyze the occurrence rate of adverse events in CSII pump versus MDI users (both user groups are part of the experimental/intensive treatment group). tables.description: The dataset represents variables used to assess volunteers' understanding of the material presented to them during eligibility screening (and in the handbook), e.g. understanding of the disease, purpose of DCCT, what to expect as a volunteer, and differences between treatment groups.  Information was collected at eligibility screening (one questionnaire per person, although a few volunteers may have completed more than one questionnaire).

tables.description: The dataset represents variables used for comparison of the occurrence of macrovascular disease and risk factors (e.g. cerebro-vascular disease, coronary arterial disease, peripheral vascular disease, death) in DCCT volunteers randomized to the standard versus those randomized to the experimental group (for published results see <a href="http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7732997?ordinalpos=2&itool=EntrezSystem2.PEntrez.Pubmed.Pubmed_ResultsPanel.Pubmed_DefaultReportPanel.Pubmed_RVDocSum"> PMID 7732997</a>).  Average follow-up time was 6.5 years.  (2)  Repeated measures of putative risk factors (see also: (1)  Detailed Record of macrovascular events, pht000231, ms5exprt)
